<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/03-locking/for-update-blocks.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

```
A> BEGIN;
BEGIN

A> SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE id = 1 FOR UPDATE;
 id | owner | balance 
----+-------+---------
  1 | alice |     100 
(1 row)
```

*Reading the locked row costs B nothing — MVCC readers don't take row locks.*

```
B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)
```

*Writing to it is another story: B's UPDATE must wait for A.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 10 WHERE id = 1;
⏳ B is waiting for a lock…

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 150 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

A> COMMIT;
COMMIT

⏵ B resumes:
UPDATE 1

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     140 
(1 row)
```

<small>Verified against PostgreSQL 18.4 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/postgres-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/03-locking/for-update-blocks.ts)</small>
